title: Python documentation
description:
One you feel comfortable with a tool’s documentation, it unlocks a new level of capabilities.

Python’s documentation is generally good, but getting comfortable with it takes some effort.

This month, Chris May will show us around the documentation and show tools that help him find what he needs fast and surface new options to get things done.

You’ll walk away with some resources that will help you on your programming journey and more ability to find what you need.

**Schedule**
5:45 pm - Doors Open / Social
6:30 pm - Meeting Start
7:30 pm - Wrap-up
8:00 pm - Prologue (Social in Scotts Addition)

**All events are open to all skill levels**. If you're just starting out, come to learn and feel free to ask questions. That's where the real learning happens. If you're a more seasoned developer, we'd love to learn from you. Despite what the Zen of Python says, there are usually more than one way to do something, so share your knowledge!

**Free parking** is available in the surface level parking lots on either side of the Ippon building.

Join our Discord server! **https://pyrva.org/discord**
Want to sponsor PyRVA? **https://pyrva.org/donate**
Want to present? **https://forms.gle/q8w3xziArWjud5f67**
